desc: Architecture and process decisions @sheldon should default to. Captures structural patterns proven in the AIOSON framework itself. Extend after each architectural review.

nodes.title: workspace/template parity for agent files
nodes.s: Every change to .aioson/agents/*.md must be mirrored in template/.aioson/agents/*.md. The sync direction is template→workspace (sync:agents copies template to workspace), so new content must be propagated to template manually or it is silently lost on next sync.
nodes.p:
// after editing .aioson/agents/X.md, also Edit template/.aioson/agents/X.md
// verify with: diff -q .aioson/agents/X.md template/.aioson/agents/X.md
nodes.not: edit only the workspace copy — the template stays stale and the next sync wipes the change
nodes.warn: some pairs have legitimate preexisting diffs (e.g., qa.md feature:close logic) — review the diff before propagating, do not blindly mirror

nodes.title: classification gates scale process depth
nodes.s: Every project/feature gets a MICRO/SMALL/MEDIUM classification that gates which agents run and which artefacts are produced. MICRO = product→dev. SMALL = product→analyst→dev. MEDIUM = full chain with all gates. Skipping the classification step bloats MICRO work and starves MEDIUM work.
nodes.p:
1. @analyst runs first to classify
2. The classification routes to the appropriate agent chain
3. Process depth (artefacts, gates, reviews) follows the classification, not the urgency
nodes.not: running the full MEDIUM pipeline on every task because 'it's safer' — kills throughput on MICRO work
nodes.warn: do not let user urgency reclassify down — urgency does not shrink risk surface

nodes.title: strict context sandbox for impartial validators
nodes.s: Agents whose role is to verify (validator, qa) operate best with a strict read-list, not the full conversation context. The Nautilus Pattern: validator reads only harness-contract.json, progress.json, and listed completed_steps — never other agents' artefacts or chat history. Continuity hallucinations vanish.
nodes.not: feeding the validator the full conversation history — it starts agreeing with prior agents instead of checking the binary contract
nodes.warn: rules from .aioson/rules/ may add criteria but never expand the sandbox — validator stays read-restricted

nodes.title: discovery before architecture — never the reverse
nodes.s: discovery.md (entities, business rules, constraints) must precede architecture.md (modules, stack choices, file layout). Architecting against assumed entities reliably ships the wrong abstraction.
nodes.not: architect.md written before discovery.md — entities get reverse-engineered from the architecture diagram, business rules get lost
nodes.warn: in brownfield projects, discovery still comes first but it is reverse discovery (scan code → discovery.md), not greenfield interview

nodes.title: integrate via CLI command, not direct file write
nodes.s: When an agent needs to update a structured artefact (dossier, project-pulse, features.md, current-state.md), prefer the CLI command (dossier:add-finding, pulse:update, feature:close) over hand-editing the file. The CLI enforces schema, updates indices, and emits telemetry the dashboard needs.
nodes.src: AIOSON audit Lote B (discovery-design-doc, validator, tester dossier integration)
nodes.not: rewriting an artefact's whole structure to add one entry — breaks index sync and loses telemetry
nodes.warn: the CLI fallback path (manual edit) must remain available when CLI is missing — agents that hard-require CLI become unusable in plain Claude Code sessions
